SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper states its main claims and provides formal objectives (Eqs. 4,5,8), describes architectures and training details, and evaluates on standard benchmarks (STL-10, PASCAL) with reasonable baselines (supervised VGG-A', SSL-GAN, Pathak et al.). The experimental methodology is largely sound (multiple STL-10 folds, comparison to prior methods) and the conclusions are consistent with reported gains. Missing, however, are detailed quantitative tables and ablation statistics in the provided text (e.g. exact numbers, confidence intervals for most results) and explicit statistical tests; some architectural/implementation details are relegated to figures referenced but not shown. These are minor issues that do not undermine the main technical claims.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The idea of using in-painting/context as an unsupervised signal is not new and the paper explicitly relates to contemporaneous work (Pathak et al., 2016) and other context-based methods (Doersch et al.). The main novelty is the specific combination of context-conditional in-painting with an adversarial discriminator used as a feature extractor (and the CC-GAN2 variant combining full-image and in-painted negatives), and demonstrating it scales to larger discriminators (VGG). Overall this is a moderate, non-trivial extension of existing techniques rather than a wholly new paradigm.

CLARITY.justification: The paper follows a logical structure (introduction, related work, method, experiments, discussion) and defines objectives and architectures clearly (objectives Eqs. 1–8; description of generator/discriminator). However, the provided text contains duplicated and garbled passages and references to figures/tables that are not present here, which impairs readability and makes it hard to verify some implementation details and quantitative results. Notation is generally consistent where present, but the presentation would benefit from cleaner formatting and inclusion of the referenced figures/tables.
